assessor_1_raw: Dense embeddings vs. BM25 for support retrieval: well-designed experiment. Temporal split with product area stratification is appropriate. Weak-label audit (1,000 pairs manually annotated) directly calibrates label quality. Strict label (click + no rejection) mitigates popularity bias. Hard negative mining from BM25 is standard practice. NDCG@5 with per-product-area analysis enables confound detection. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: The experiment is methodologically sound. Data quality controls (removing bot interactions, short queries, unpopular articles) are appropriate. The strict secondary label is a principled approach to reduce popularity bias. Fit-apply separation is explicitly specified. Coverage secondary metric addresses the no-candidate problem. Popularity correlation (Spearman) is an excellent confound check. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: Well-designed retrieval experiment with comprehensive confound controls. The Spearman rank correlation between model ranking and article click volume directly tests the popularity matching concern. Per-product-area NDCG@5 detects concentrated gains. Temporal drift analysis (months 19-21 vs. 22-24) is a strong robustness check. Test set policy is rigorous. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: All three assessors unanimous: defense_wins. Consistent evaluation.
